mission.title: Install Porter CLI
mission.description: Porter is a client-side CLI tool that implements the Cloud Native Application Bundle (CNAB) specification. It enables you to package your application artifacts, client tools, configuration, and deployment logic into a single installer bundle. Porter is NOT a Kubernetes deployment -- it is a local CLI tool installed on your workstation.

steps.title: Install Porter CLI
steps.description:
Install Porter on your workstation using the official install script.

**macOS:**
```bash
curl -L https://cdn.porter.sh/latest/install-mac.sh | bash
```

**Linux:**
```bash
curl -L https://cdn.porter.sh/latest/install-linux.sh | bash
```

**Windows (PowerShell):**
```powershell
iwr "https://cdn.porter.sh/latest/install-windows.ps1" -UseBasicParsing | iex
```

The installer places the porter binary in ~/.porter and adds it to your PATH.

steps.title: Verify the installation
steps.description:
Verify that Porter is installed correctly:

```bash
porter version
```

You should see the installed version number.

steps.title: Install mixins (optional)
steps.description:
Porter uses mixins to interact with different tools. Install commonly used mixins:

```bash
porter mixin install helm3
porter mixin install kubernetes
porter mixin install terraform
```

List installed mixins:

```bash
porter mixin list
```

steps.title: Create your first bundle (optional)
steps.description:
Create a new Porter bundle project to get started:

```bash
porter create my-bundle
cd my-bundle
```

This creates a porter.yaml file and supporting files that define your bundle.

uninstall.title: Remove Porter CLI
uninstall.description:
Remove Porter by deleting the installation directory:

```bash
rm -r ~/.porter
```

Also remove Porter from your PATH by editing your shell profile (~/.bashrc, ~/.zshrc, etc.) and removing the Porter PATH entry.

upgrade.title: Upgrade Porter CLI
upgrade.description:
Re-run the install script to upgrade to the latest version:

**macOS:**
```bash
curl -L https://cdn.porter.sh/latest/install-mac.sh | bash
```

**Linux:**
```bash
curl -L https://cdn.porter.sh/latest/install-linux.sh | bash
```

Verify the new version:
```bash
porter version
```

troubleshooting.title: porter command not found
troubleshooting.description:
If the porter command is not found after installation, ensure ~/.porter is in your PATH. Add it to your shell profile:

```bash
export PATH="$HOME/.porter:$PATH"
```

Then reload your shell:
```bash
source ~/.bashrc  # or ~/.zshrc
```

troubleshooting.title: Permission denied during installation
troubleshooting.description:
If you get a permission denied error, set a custom install directory:

```bash
export PORTER_HOME=/path/to/custom/dir
curl -L https://cdn.porter.sh/latest/install-linux.sh | bash
```

troubleshooting.title: Mixin install fails
troubleshooting.description:
If a mixin installation fails, check your network connection and try again. You can also install a specific version:

```bash
porter mixin install helm3 --version v1.0.0
```

resolution.summary: A successful installation of Porter will have the porter CLI available in your PATH. Porter is a client-side tool, not a Kubernetes deployment. You can verify the installation by running 'porter version' and then use Porter to create, build, and install CNAB bundles.

prerequisites.description: Porter is a client-side CLI tool. You need curl (macOS/Linux) or PowerShell (Windows) to download and install it. Docker is needed if you plan to build bundles.
